commit.message:
fix(hot-reload): register NuGet and framework assembly search paths

Register NuGet package directories and framework SDK directories from
OtherOptions -r: args in assemblySearchPaths during mkReloadingState.
Previously only project output directories were registered, causing
FileNotFoundException for transitive NuGet dependencies (e.g.
OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.AspNetCore) when loading real-world
projects inside SageFs FSI.

Chesterton's fence: The assembly resolver searches assemblySearchPaths
for missing DLLs at runtime. Without NuGet dirs, any transitive
dependency not in the project's own bin/ fails to resolve — blocking
projects with deep dependency trees from running inside SageFs.

Add 5 property tests covering registerSearchPath behavior for
project outputs, NuGet packages, framework SDK paths, and dedup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

3. Binary Translator - decode binary to words
4. Speed Typer - type AI terms against the clock
5. Odd One Out - spot what doesn't belong
6. Memory Match - pair terms with definitions
7. AI Timeline - sort milestones chronologically
8. Bias Detective - spot AI bias in scenarios
9. Token Counter - guess how LLMs tokenise text

Features:
- Random game on each visit with 'New Game' button
- Browse all games in a compact grid picker
- All mobile-friendly with touch-optimised buttons
- Educational — every game teaches real AI concepts

feat: add enable-storage-compat emitter option (#3814)

* feat: add enable-storage-compat emitter option

Add a new emitter flag `enable-storage-compat` (default: false) that augments
every regular (non-LRO, non-paging) operation return type with a `_response`
property containing:
- `rawResponse`: PipelineResponse captured via the onResponse callback
- `parsedBody`: the deserialized response body
- `parsedHeaders`: the deserialized response headers

Implementation uses a static helper pattern (storageCompatResponse.ts) with:
- `StorageCompatResponseInfo<TBody, THeaders>` type
- `createStorageCompatOnResponse()` to intercept the PipelineResponse
- `addStorageCompatResponse()` to augment the return value

Files changed:
- lib.ts, interfaces.ts, transfromRLCOptions.ts: option definition and plumbing
- static-helpers-metadata.ts, index.ts: helper registration and conditional loading
- operationHelpers.ts: operation generation with onResponse injection
- storageCompatResponse.ts: new static helper
- docs/storage-compat-response.md: feature design documentation
- Scenario tests and unit tests for full coverage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: change rawResponse type from PipelineResponse to FullOperationResponse

FullOperationResponse extends PipelineResponse and is the actual type
received by the onResponse callback. This provides access to additional
properties like rawHeaders and parsedBody on the raw response object.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* response

* Skip headers with headerCollectionPrefix clientOption in serialization/deserialization

Add shouldSkipHeaderSerialization() predicate that checks getClientOptions(header,
"headerCollectionPrefix") to filter headers at 4 collection points:
- Request headers in getHeaderAndBodyParameters()
- Response headers in getResponseHeaders()
- Exception headers in getExceptionResponseHeaders()
- Composite response types in buildCompositeResponseType()

Headers with this option are excluded from ser/deser codegen but remain in
model/type definitions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: clarify session destroy vs delete semantics (#599)

* docs: clarify session destroy vs delete semantics across all SDKs

Clarify the distinction between destroy() (closes session, releases
in-memory resources, preserves disk state for resumption) and
deleteSession() (permanently removes all data from disk).

Update doc comments across all four SDK languages (Go, Node.js, Python,
.NET) and the session persistence guide to make the behavioral
difference explicit and help users choose the right method.

Fixes #526

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add disconnect() method, deprecate destroy() across all SDKs

Add disconnect() as the preferred method for closing sessions across all
four SDK languages, marking destroy() as deprecated:

- Node.js: disconnect() + Symbol.asyncDispose support, destroy() delegates
- Python: disconnect() + __aenter__/__aexit__ context manager, destroy()
  emits DeprecationWarning
- Go: Disconnect() method, Destroy() marked with Deprecated godoc tag
- .NET: DisconnectAsync() method, DisposeAsync() delegates to it

Update all samples, READMEs, and documentation guides to use the new
disconnect() terminology. Internal stop() methods now call disconnect().

Resolves PR #599 comments:
- Rename destroy → disconnect for clarity
- Define IDisposable behavior in .NET (DisposeAsync delegates to
  DisconnectAsync)
- Add idiomatic cleanup patterns (async context managers, Symbol.asyncDispose)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: update all tests, scenarios, and docs to use disconnect()

Migrate all test scenarios, e2e tests, READMEs, and documentation
references from destroy()/Destroy() to disconnect()/Disconnect().

- 90 test scenario files across Go/Python/TypeScript/C#
- 15 Node.js e2e test files
- 8 Python e2e test files
- 3 Go e2e test files
- 1 .NET test file
- READMEs and compatibility docs updated with new API reference
- Agent docs updated with new method names
- Reconnect scenario log messages updated to 'disconnected'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: rename snapshot to match updated test name

The hooks_extended test 'should invoke onSessionEnd hook when session is
destroyed' was renamed to '...disconnected', but the snapshot YAML file
wasn't renamed to match, causing CI to fail with 'No cached response'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* dotnet: remove DisconnectAsync, keep only DisposeAsync

Address review feedback from SteveSandersonMS: for .NET, the
standard IAsyncDisposable pattern (DisposeAsync) is sufficient
on its own without a duplicate DisconnectAsync method.

Moves the disconnect implementation directly into DisposeAsync
and removes the separate DisconnectAsync method. Updates all
references in Client.cs and README.md accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(opencode): add secure permissions config to block dangerous commands (#334)

* feat(opencode): add secure permissions config to block dangerous commands

Add comprehensive deny rules to OpenCode configuration requiring user
confirmation before executing destructive or dangerous commands. The deny
list covers 129 command patterns across multiple categories:

- Destructive file ops (rm -rf, rmdir)
- Privilege escalation (sudo, su)
- System control (kill, shutdown, reboot)
- Disk operations (dd, mkfs, fdisk, diskutil)
- Piped execution (curl|sh, wget|bash)
- Git destructive ops (force push, reset --hard, clean, branch -D)
- Docker (rm, prune, system prune, volume/network cleanup)
- Kubernetes (delete, drain, cordon, apply, exec, scale)
- Helm (install, upgrade, uninstall, rollback)
- Terraform (apply, destroy, state rm, taint)
- Package publishing (npm/yarn/pnpm/cargo/gem publish)
- macOS system (defaults write/delete, launchctl, networksetup)
- Cloud CLI (aws, gcloud, az destructive operations)
- Remote access (ssh, scp, rsync --delete)

Closes #333

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(opencode): rewrite permissions deny rules using regex patterns

Replaces glob-style patterns (e.g., `sudo *`) with proper regex patterns
(e.g., `^sudo\b`) as expected by OpenCode's permissions system. The
previous glob patterns would not match correctly when interpreted as
regex - for example, `*` in regex is a quantifier, not a wildcard, and
`|` is alternation rather than a literal pipe.

Key improvements:
- Use `\b` word boundaries for precise command matching
- Use `\s+` for whitespace matching instead of literal spaces
- Escape regex metacharacters (`$`, `(`, `)`, `|`, `.`, `*`)
- Use `^` anchors where commands must start at the beginning
- Consolidate similar patterns with alternation groups
- Reduce from 129 to 97 patterns while maintaining the same coverage
- Fix misleading "Zen provider" wording in Ansible task names

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(opencode): use correct permission schema with glob patterns

Rewrites OpenCode permissions config to match official documentation:
- Changed key from "permissions" (plural) to "permission" (singular)
- Changed structure from { deny: { bash: [...] } } to { bash: { pattern: action } }
- Converted regex patterns to glob-style wildcards (* for any chars)
- Use "deny" for truly destructive commands (rm -rf /, terraform destroy)
- Use "ask" for commands that need confirmation (kubectl, docker prune)
- Added "*": "allow" as base rule with last-match-wins semantics

References: https://opencode.ai/docs/permissions/

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(opencode): add comprehensive cloud CLI deny/ask rules

Expand OpenCode permissions from ~100 to 293 patterns with deep coverage
of gcloud, aws, and az CLI commands:

- gcloud: 58 new patterns covering Compute, GKE, Cloud SQL, IAM, KMS,
  Pub/Sub, Cloud Functions, Cloud Run, DNS, Secret Manager, App Engine,
  Spanner, Firestore, Cloud Storage, Artifact Registry, Redis,
  Composer, BigQuery (bq), and logging/monitoring
- aws: 47 new patterns covering EC2, EKS, RDS, IAM, Lambda, DynamoDB,
  SNS/SQS, Secrets Manager, ECS/ECR, Route53, CloudFront, ElastiCache,
  Redshift, EFS, Cognito, Kinesis, KMS, and OpenSearch
- az: 52 new patterns covering VMs, AKS, SQL/MySQL/PostgreSQL, Cosmos DB,
  Storage, Key Vault, Networking, DNS, App Services, APIM, Service Bus,
  Event Hubs, monitoring, Redis, ACR, and RBAC/AD

Design: "deny" for irreversible catastrophic ops (project/org deletion,
KMS key destruction, force-delete with data loss), "ask" for potentially
dangerous but often legitimate operations.

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(opencode): add wildcard catch-all delete patterns and gsutil support

Add catch-all wildcard patterns as safety nets for all cloud CLI tools:
- "gcloud * delete*", "gcloud * destroy*", "gcloud * remove*" → ask
- "aws * delete-*", "aws * remove-*", "aws * terminate-*", "aws * purge-*" → ask
- "az * delete*", "az * purge*" → ask

These patterns intercept any delete/destroy command even for subcommands
not explicitly listed, since "last matching rule wins" in OpenCode.

Add comprehensive gsutil patterns (legacy GCS CLI):
- gsutil rm -r/-a/-ra/-ar, gsutil rb → deny (destructive, irreversible)
- gsutil rm, mv, cp -r, rsync -d, acl, iam, lifecycle, etc. → ask

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(opencode): implement review feedback for permissions config

- Change all git deny rules to ask (force push, reset --hard, clean,
  checkout, branch -D are local-only and used in everyday workflows)
- Add *command* wildcard catch-all patterns for kubectl, terraform,
  helm, and rm to catch wrapped commands (e.g. just run, make run)
- Add rm -rf . and rm -rf .. deny rules for current/parent directory
  protection
- Add spaced pipe variants for curl/wget piped to sh/bash
- Fix docker stop/rm patterns to use generic wildcards instead of
  broken $() shell syntax
- Fix az role assignment delete dead code by moving deny rule after
  the wildcard ask rule (last matching rule wins)
- Update Ansible debug message to reflect both deny and ask behaviors
- Update changelog to reflect 337 total permission rules

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(opencode): compress permissions config from 337 to 175 rules

Leverage wildcard catch-all patterns to eliminate redundant rules:
- Cloud CLIs: "gcloud * delete*", "aws * delete-*", "az * delete*"
  catch-alls make individual service-specific "ask" rules redundant,
  keeping only "deny" overrides for critical operations
- kubectl/helm/terraform: double-wildcard catch-alls (*kubectl delete*)
  already cover direct invocations, remove duplicates
- Fix ordering bug: "*rm -rf*" (ask) must come BEFORE "*rm -rf /*" (deny)
  so the deny override wins for slash-prefixed paths
- Compress curl/wget pipe patterns from 8 to 4 (glob * absorbs spaces)
- Merge security delete-keychain/certificate into security delete-*
- Merge gsutil acl/defacl into gsutil *acl*

48% reduction in rules with identical security coverage.

Co-authored-by: Paolo Mainardi <paolomainardi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
consensus: fix VRF eligibility and reward address routing

VRF sortition used the previous block's bitmap as a hard gate for
eligibility. High-latency nodes whose precommit votes arrived after
advance_vote_height cleared the accumulator were systematically
excluded from the bitmap and therefore from VRF selection, causing
them to earn significantly fewer block-producer rewards (30 TIME).

Fix: VRF sortition now uses all currently-active masternodes as the
candidate pool. The bitmap still gates pool rewards via
consensus_participants_bitmap in blockchain.rs, preserving the
participation incentive for paid tiers.

Also fix: masternode re-registration did not overwrite
existing.masternode.wallet_address when reward_address changed in
time.conf. Block rewards use wallet_address, so the stale value caused
rewards to route to the old local wallet instead of the configured
GUI wallet. Added the missing wallet_address sync in register_internal.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(nostr): pass explicit retry options to ndk-blossom uploads (#58)

* fix(nostr): pass explicit retry options to ndk-blossom upload

ndk-blossom v2.0.2 has a bug where uploadToServer passes
options.maxRetries (undefined) to fetchWithRetry, which
spreads over DEFAULT_RETRY_OPTIONS and overrides maxRetries
with undefined. This causes the while loop condition
(attempts <= undefined) to be false, so the fetch never
executes at all.

Fix by explicitly passing maxRetries: 3 and retryDelay: 1000
in the upload options.

commit.message:
Add get_loc_stats.sh: scalable LOC stats for orgs/enterprises (#7)

* Add get_loc_stats.sh: scalable LOC stats for orgs/enterprises

Collects lines added/deleted per contributor across an entire GitHub
organization or enterprise using batched GraphQL queries. Supports
GitHub CLI and GitHub App authentication modes.

Features:
- Batched GraphQL queries (configurable repos per call) for scalability
- Dual auth: gh CLI (5K req/hr) or GitHub App JWT (15K req/hr)
- Dual scope: --org or --enterprise
- Per-contributor and per-org aggregation in CSV and/or JSON
- Rate-limit aware with automatic sleep/retry
- --test and --max-repos flags for development/testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review comments

- Extract OS-aware date helpers (get_past_date, date_to_epoch) to
  reduce duplication between SINCE_DATE calculation and rate limit check
- Remove stderr suppression (2>/dev/null) from gh api graphql call so
  auth/network errors are visible for debugging
- Fix misleading alias comment and remove unnecessary tr on repo alias
  (alias is already repo_${idx}, contains no hyphens)
- Use commit author name/email as fallback instead of generic 'unknown'
  for commits without a linked GitHub user account
- Coerce null additions/deletions to 0 via jq (// 0) to prevent null
  sums from merge commits or binary file changes
- Add input validation for --days, --batch-size, and --max-repos to
  ensure they are positive integers
